commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix BTR patch: use IPlayer instead of Player, add IsPlayerEnemy patch

The previous AddEnemy patch with BTRControllerClass.Instance check never
worked reliably. Replaced with two patches:

- IsPlayerEnemy: prevents BTR group from marking anyone as enemy, and
  prevents other groups from marking the BTR shooter as enemy
- AddEnemy: same logic as fallback for alternative enemy-adding paths

Fixed critical crash caused by Harmony prefix declaring Player instead
of IPlayer for a method that takes IPlayer, causing a native JIT crash
(KERNELBASE.dll 0xe0000001) that no try/catch could intercept.

commit.message:
kernel/BDE/init: I2C MPC support, SCHAN diagnostics, HW default docs, Jekyll Pages

- remote-build.sh: add CONFIG_I2C_MPC (P2020 I2C bus driver), set
  CONFIG_LOCALVERSION="-nos" and disable CONFIG_LOCALVERSION_AUTO to
  fix "5.10.0-dirty" kernel version string
- rootfs/build.sh: update default KERNEL_VERSION to 5.10.0-nos
- nos_kernel_bde.c: add SCHAN diagnostic logging and PIO init in probe
  (logs SCHAN_CTRL, ring_cfg, ring_addr, ring_head, dma_cfg at load time)
- init.c: remove warm_boot early return gate; always run full init
  sequence (SBUS ring maps, LINK40G, SCHAN clear, XLPORT deassert);
  add DMA ring diagnostic logging (0x10c, 0x400)
- bcm56846_regs.h: document BCM56846 HW power-on defaults (0x10c=
  0x32000043, 0x400=0x505b8d80 are always present, not DMA mode
  indicators); add CMIC_SCHAN_RING_CFG and CMIC_SCHAN_RING_HEAD defines
- docs/: add Jekyll _config.yml and index.md for GitHub Pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(hooks): replace claude -p review subprocess with inline CONTINUE prompt

The previous approach spawned a headless `claude -p` subprocess to score
completed goals. This caused two problems:
1. OOM kill — the Node.js process consumed ~3.6GB on 3.8GB VMs, killing
   the entire claude-worker service cgroup before reviewed_at could be written
2. Recursive stop-hook loop — the subprocess inherited the same settings.json
   and triggered its own Phase 3 review on exit

New approach: Phase 3 outputs a CONTINUE message pointing the *current*
Claude instance at the goals data. Claude reviews inline using its existing
Bash tool, writing reviewed_at and any fix goals directly. No new process,
no OOM risk, no recursion.

Removes CLAUDE_WORKER_REVIEWING guard (no longer needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve API 500 errors and complete v2 filter implementation

Bugs fixed:
- AntSpeciesDetailSerializer was missing explicit IntegerRangeField
  declarations for 5 range fields (flight_hour_range, nest_temperature,
  nest_humidity, outworld_temperature, outworld_humidity). DRF cannot
  auto-serialize psycopg2 range objects, causing 500s for any species
  with non-null range data (e.g. Lasius niger ID 298). This also
  caused all slug-based lookups to fail for affected species.
- v2 AntSpeciesDetailView used `finally:` for get_object_or_404 instead
  of placing it after the try/except block (matching v1 pattern).
- settings.STORAGES was missing the "default" key when PUBLIC_ROOT is
  set, causing InvalidStorageError when serializing image fields.

v2 features implemented:
- ?search= on /api/v2/ants/ (case-insensitive name substring filter)
- ?region= on /api/v2/ants/ (by region code or ID via distribution)
- ?month= on /api/v2/ants/nuptial-flight-months/ (by flight month ID)

OpenAPI schema improvements:
- Added @extend_schema to v2 NuptialFlightMonths for manual query params
- Added serializer_class and @extend_schema to v2 AntSpeciesDetailView
- Added labels to AntSpeciesFilter fields for schema descriptions
- Added return type hints to SerializerMethodField methods

Tests: 47 tests covering all bugs and new features

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- All operations properly handle C++ memory with defer free()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add context manager support with HTTP connection pooling (#117)

## Summary

- Enable `with DataverseClient(...) as client:` for automatic resource
cleanup and HTTP connection pooling
- Add `close()` method for explicit lifecycle management (idempotent,
safe to call multiple times)
- Thread `requests.Session` through `DataverseClient` → `_ODataClient` →
`_HttpClient` for TCP/TLS reuse
- Guard all operations against use-after-close via `_check_closed()` in
`_scoped_odata()` — raises `RuntimeError("DataverseClient is closed")`
- Clear all internal caches (entity set, primary ID, picklist) on close
- Full backward compatibility: client works identically without `with`
statement

### Files changed
- `src/PowerPlatform/Dataverse/core/_http.py` — session parameter +
`close()`
- `src/PowerPlatform/Dataverse/data/_odata.py` — session threading +
`close()` with cache clearing
- `src/PowerPlatform/Dataverse/client.py` —
`__enter__`/`__exit__`/`close()`/`_check_closed()`
- `tests/unit/test_context_manager.py` — 34 new tests (protocol, session
lifecycle, close behavior, closed-state guards, backward compat,
exception handling)

## Test plan
- [x] 34 new unit tests covering all context manager behavior
- [x] Full test suite passes (195/195)
- [ ] Manual verification with live Dataverse environment

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Support additional okteto manifest filename patterns (#287)

* Support additional okteto manifest filename patterns

Expand manifest file discovery to include:
- okteto-*.yml, okteto-*.yaml (e.g., okteto-stack.yml)
- okteto.*.yml, okteto.*.yaml (e.g., okteto.dev.yml, okteto.prod.yaml)

For deploy commands, any file matching these patterns is now supported.
For up commands, only exact matches (okteto.yml, okteto.yaml, docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.yaml) are allowed.

Updated glob pattern and added pattern matching logic to filter appropriately based on command type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add comprehensive unit tests for manifest pattern matching

Refactored pattern matching logic into exported isManifestSupported function
for better testability.

New test coverage (14 tests, +27% increase):
- Deploy command patterns (allowPatterns=true)
  - Exact matches from supported list
  - okteto-*.{yml,yaml} patterns
  - okteto.*.{yml,yaml} patterns
  - Rejection of non-matching files
  - Edge cases with multiple dots/dashes
- Up command patterns (allowPatterns=false)
  - Exact matches only
  - Rejection of all pattern-based files
- Case sensitivity validation
- Empty and invalid input handling

Test results:
- 66 unit tests passing (was 52)
- 10 E2E tests passing
- Extension packages successfully (1.61 MB)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add comprehensive documentation for manifest patterns

Updated documentation:
- CHANGELOG.md: Added feature description and updated test count
- docs/manifest-patterns.md: New comprehensive guide covering:
  - All supported patterns and use cases
  - Command-specific behavior (Up vs Deploy)
  - Examples and best practices
  - Troubleshooting guide
  - Migration guide
- docs/index.md: Added link to manifest patterns documentation
- README.md: Added supported manifest files section
- TESTING.md: Added test cases for custom manifest patterns

Documentation covers:
- Pattern matching rules (okteto-*.yml, okteto.*.yml)
- Differences between Up and Deploy commands
- Use cases (environment-specific, feature branches, components)
- Multiple manifest handling
- Best practices and troubleshooting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Unify manifest pattern support across all commands

All Okteto commands (Up, Deploy, Destroy, Test) now support the same
manifest patterns, removing previous restrictions on the Up command.

Changes:
- Removed allowPatterns parameter from isManifestSupported()
- All commands now accept okteto-*.{yml,yaml} and okteto.*.{yml,yaml} patterns
- Enables using environment-specific manifests (okteto.dev.yml) with Up command
- Simplified code and unified behavior across all commands

Updated tests (63 passing):
- Reorganized test cases to reflect unified behavior
- All pattern tests now verify support across all command types
- Removed command-specific filtering tests

Updated documentation:
- CHANGELOG.md: Updated feature description
- docs/manifest-patterns.md: Removed Up command restrictions
- README.md: Clarified all commands support all patterns
- TESTING.md: Updated test cases for unified behavior

Benefits:
- Simpler mental model - one set of rules for all commands
- More flexible workflows - use custom patterns with any command
- Consistent user experience across extension

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix extension test merge artifacts after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Close SQL and Redis connections after integration tests

Bun.sql and Bun.redis create connection pools that persist after
the test runner exits, leaving zombie connections that accumulate
across repeated runs until PostgreSQL hits max_connections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix perma-loading: render data progressively as each source resolves

Replace Promise.allSettled (blocks on slowest) with parallel .then() callbacks.
Chart meta renders immediately, v7 and fundamentals update the panel as they
arrive. User sees price/52wk/volume within ~1s regardless of API slowness.

Fix selfmod proposal parsing: use delimited format instead of JSON

JSON breaks when LLM embeds full file contents (unescaped newlines/quotes).
Switched to ===PROPOSAL===  / ===FILE=== / ===END=== delimited format with
JSON fallback. Also: bump max_tokens 4096 -> 16384, add retry on parse
failure, and send only goal-relevant files instead of entire codebase.

Hero image selection in identifyDownloadableUrls had no icon/decorative filtering
or minimum size checks, allowing SVGs, small icons, and decorative images to be
selected as the hero. Adds isLikelyIconOrDecorative() and hasHeroDimensions()
guards to all 3 hero detection paths (header scan, section fallback, last resort).
